metadata.title: Azents — AI Agents for Your Team
metadata.description: Build and deploy AI agents that automate your team's routine work. No coding required.

hero.headline: Stop delegating to humans.
hero.subheadline:
Create custom AI agents in minutes — no coding required.
Code review, ticket triage, metrics, reporting — so your team can focus on what actually matters.

features.headline: Build the agents your team actually needs.

teamAgents.title: Built for Teams
teamAgents.description: Agents that understand your team's context, projects, and preferences. Not a personal assistant — a shared teammate.

uiBuilder.title: Visual Agent Builder
uiBuilder.description: Design agents with drag-and-drop. Define skills, set triggers, connect tools — zero engineering required.

batteryIncluded.title: Ready-Made Skills
batteryIncluded.description: 30+ built-in skills from code review to meeting summaries. Start automating in minutes, not weeks.

security.title: Privacy & Security
security.description: SSO, audit logs, RBAC, and data isolation out of the box. Your data never leaves your team.

automation.title: Always-On Automation
automation.description: Set it once, it runs 24/7. Monitoring, reporting, and acting — even while you sleep.

multiAgent.title: Multi-Agent Collaboration
multiAgent.description: Agents hand off tasks to each other. A code reviewer flags an issue, a doc writer updates the spec, a notifier pings the team.

useCases.headline: Still assigning this to a human?
useCases.subheadline: Here's what changes when an agent takes over.

design.title: Design
design.tagline: Automated research — designers focus on creating
design.description: Your design team spends hours browsing Dribbble, Behance, and competitor apps. Azents crawls them automatically and builds mood boards in minutes.
design.before: An intern screenshots 50+ pages manually over 2 hours

marketing.title: Marketing
marketing.tagline: Real-time brand monitoring across every channel
marketing.description: After launch, who's watching Reddit, X, ProductHunt, and HackerNews? Azents monitors in real time and surfaces what actually matters.
marketing.before: An intern manually collects mentions from Reddit and X all day

operations.title: Operations
operations.tagline: Automated documentation the moment a meeting ends
operations.description: Writing notes, tracking decisions, listing action items — important but tedious. Azents handles everything from recording to structured docs.
operations.before: 30 minutes writing notes after every 1-hour meeting
operations.after: Summary, decisions, and action items in Notion instantly

engineering.title: Engineering
engineering.tagline: Instant first-pass review on every new PR
engineering.description: Review backlogs, stale docs, repetitive boilerplate — Azents removes the bottlenecks slowing your engineering team down.
engineering.before: PRs pile up waiting for a senior reviewer
engineering.after: Bugs, security issues, style violations — first-pass review posted instantly

skills.headline: Build once, the whole team uses it.
skills.description: Skills are reusable recipes for your agents. Anyone on the team can create one — no coding required.

cta.headline: Stop delegating to humans.
cta.subheadline: Your first agent is just a few minutes away.

chatPreview.userMessage: Launch the Q2 campaign across all channels

errors.handleFormat: Only lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens allowed

llmSettings.headline: LLM Integrations
llmSettings.description: Manage API keys and credentials for LLM providers used in this workspace.
llmSettings.apiKeyPlaceholderEdit: Leave empty to keep current key
llmSettings.credentialsPlaceholderEdit: Leave empty to keep current value
llmSettings.setupGuideApiKey: Go to the provider's dashboard, create an API key, and paste it above.
llmSettings.setupGuideAwsStep1: 1. Create an IAM user (or use an existing one) in the AWS Console.
llmSettings.setupGuideAwsStep3: 3. Create an Access Key for the user.
llmSettings.setupGuideAwsStep4: 4. Enter the Access Key ID, Secret Access Key, and region above.
llmSettings.setupGuideAwsRoleAssume: 5. (Cross-account access) To use Bedrock in another AWS account, enter the Role ARN. The IAM user must have sts:AssumeRole permission, and the target role must include this account in its trust policy.
llmSettings.setupGuideAwsStep5: 6. (First-time activation for AWS Marketplace models) For third-party models that require a Marketplace subscription (e.g., Meta, Mistral), the IAM user with the above permissions must invoke the model once directly. After the first invocation, the model is enabled account-wide for all users.
llmSettings.setupGuideAwsStep6: 7. (Still failing?) If you are using AWS Organizations, a Service Control Policy (SCP) at the OU or Org level may be blocking aws-marketplace actions. Ask your organization administrator to review the SCPs.
llmSettings.setupGuideGcpStep1: 1. Create a service account in the GCP Console.
llmSettings.setupGuideGcpStep3: 3. Create a JSON key for the service account.
llmSettings.setupGuideGcpStep4: 4. Enter the Project ID, region, and JSON key above.

chatgptOAuth.title: ChatGPT subscription
chatgptOAuth.description: Connect a ChatGPT subscription account with device code.
chatgptOAuth.callbackUnavailable: Browser callback sign-in is unavailable because OpenAI rejects the Codex public client when it uses a hosted callback URL.
chatgptOAuth.deviceInstruction: Open <link>https://auth.openai.com/codex/device</link> and enter the code below.

modelSelection.title: Workspace default models
modelSelection.description: Agents copy these model snapshots when they are saved without an explicit model selection.
modelSelection.lightweightDescription: Leave empty to use the main default model.

modelConfig.headline: Model configs
modelConfig.label: Label
modelConfig.providerDefaultDescription: Leave empty to use the provider default.
modelConfig.reasoningTemperatureDefault: Reasoning effort uses the provider default temperature.
modelConfig.stopSequencesDescription: Leave empty to use the provider default. Press Enter after each sequence. Up to 4 sequences.
modelConfig.affectsAgents: This affects all agents that use this model config.
modelConfig.deleteBlocked: This affects all agents that use this model config. Delete is blocked while agents reference it.
modelConfig.referencedBy: Referenced by {count, plural, =0 {no agents} =1 {1 agent} other {# agents}}
modelConfig.parameterStopSequences: {count, plural, =1 {1 stop sequence} other {# stop sequences}}

agents.headline: Agents
agents.description: Manage AI agents for your workspace
agents.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this agent?
agents.descriptionPlaceholder: Briefly describe what this agent does
agents.mainModelDescription: Leave empty to copy the workspace default model at save time.
agents.lightweightModelDescription: Optional. Leave empty to copy the workspace lightweight default, or main model fallback.
agents.inheritModelDescription: Use the calling parent agent's LLM provider and model. Model parameters (temperature, reasoning effort, etc.) still come from this subagent.
agents.systemPromptPlaceholder: Define the agent's role and behavior guidelines
agents.maxTurnsDescription: Optional. Stop the run after this many agent turns. Leave empty for unlimited turns.
agents.lastAdminWarning: At least one admin is required
agents.noIntegrationDescription: No LLM integration has been configured. Please set up an LLM integration in Settings before creating an agent.
agents.noEnabledIntegrationDescription: Configure an enabled LLM provider integration before selecting an explicit model. Agents can still use the workspace default when it is configured.
agents.compactionModelThresholdWarning: The compaction model''s context window ({compactionMax} tokens) is smaller than the main model ({mainMax} tokens). Compaction will trigger based on the compaction model''s limit.
agents.roleAgentDescription: Can use subagents to delegate tasks
agents.roleSubagentDescription: Can be called by other agents as a tool
agents.subagentDescriptionPlaceholder: Describe when to use this subagent
agents.useParentToolkitsDescription: Inherit this parent agent's toolkits. The subagent's own toolkits are ignored for this call. Parent-only tools (memory, schedule, etc.) are excluded.
agents.shellEnabledDescription: Allow this subagent to execute shell commands
agents.memoryEnabledDescription: Allow this agent to save and recall persistent memories across conversations

sessions.title: Sessions
sessions.archiveConfirmDescription: This session will be removed from the active session list. Conversation data is kept.

danger.title: Danger Zone
danger.deleteWarning: Deleting this agent will remove all its conversations and settings. This action cannot be undone.
danger.resetRuntimeConfirm: This will discard agent workspace files and any preserved runtime state, then start an empty runtime. This action cannot be undone.

chat.empty: No conversations yet. Start one with the button above.

emptyForFilter.agent: No agents yet. Create one with the button above.
emptyForFilter.subagent: No subagents yet. Set role to "subagent" when creating an agent.

agents.selectedModelMissingDescription: The saved model snapshot may be stale or the model was removed from the latest catalog projection. Sync the catalog or select a current model before saving changes.

modelCatalogPicker.noModels: No selectable models match this search.
modelCatalogPicker.syncingWithoutSnapshot: Catalog sync is running. Models will appear after the first successful snapshot.

github.patTokenDescription: Fine-grained PAT or classic PAT with necessary permissions
github.privateKeyDescription: RSA private key in PEM format generated from the GitHub App settings
github.platformDescription: Click the button below to install the Azents GitHub App to your organization or repository. The connection will be set up automatically.
github.oauthPlatformDescription: Each user will authenticate with their own GitHub account via OAuth. No additional configuration is required — the platform OAuth App is used automatically.
github.toolsetsDescription: Select which GitHub tool groups to enable
github.credentialsSetHint: Credentials are configured. Leave fields empty to keep current values.
github.credentialsEditPlaceholder: Leave empty to keep current value
github.selectInstallationDescription: Select every account or organization this toolkit may access.
github.noInstallationsFound: No installations found. Install the GitHub App to a new organization first.
github.perUserPatDescription: Each user registers their own GitHub Personal Access Token. No workspace-level credentials are needed.
github.runtimeEnvironmentWarningTitle: Runtime environment variables — long-lived credential risk
github.runtimeEnvironmentWarningBody: When enabled, GitHub tokens are injected into the agent runtime environment. Single-installation credentials also set GH_TOKEN/GITHUB_TOKEN; multi-installation credentials set GITHUB_TOKEN_INSTALLATION_<id> plus a routing map used by the git credential helper. Full isolation is not possible — tokens can be read by any process the agent runs. Only enable if you accept this exposure risk.
github.runtimeEnvironmentToggle: Inject GitHub tokens into Runtime environment variables
github.runtimeEnvironmentToggleDescription: Requires acknowledgement above. Agents gain auto-auth for git commands. For gh commands with multi-installation credentials, use explicit token selection such as GH_TOKEN=$GITHUB_TOKEN_INSTALLATION_<id> gh ...
github.installationsDescription: Add every GitHub App installation this toolkit may use. The account login is used for tool prefixes and shell token routing.

envvar.warningBody: Values stored here will be injected into runtime shell commands as child-process env. The exposure cannot be fully prevented — prefer short-lived tokens where possible.
envvar.emptyState: No environment variables yet. Add one with the button below.
envvar.nameTooLongError: Name must be at most {max} characters
envvar.valueTooLongError: Value must be at most {max} characters

githubPat.step1Title: Step 1. Create a PAT on GitHub
githubPat.tokenSecurityNote: Your token is encrypted and stored securely. Not even the Azents team can view the raw token.
githubPat.returnToChat: Return to your chat and try again.

todo.title: Todo list

goalStatusDescription.paused: Paused by the user. Resume when you want the agent to continue automatically.
goalStatusDescription.blocked: The agent stopped because it could not make meaningful progress. Resume after the blocker is resolved.

todo.pauseGoalConfirmDescription: Automatic goal continuation will stop until you resume it.
todo.resumeGoalConfirmDescription: The agent will continue pursuing this goal from the current state.
todo.resumeHintDescription: Optional context about what changed or where to continue.
todo.resumeHintPlaceholder: e.g. The blocker is resolved; continue from the failed CI job.

context.empty: No active session yet. Start a chat to inspect context usage.
